reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop)

rubric.evidence: Real WASM run verified in headless Chrome on the index page: clicked 'Download model (~109 MB)', loader reached 'Model ready', default comment 'Shut up you pathetic waste of space…' scored offensive 99.0% in 77 ms; console clean (0 errors). transformers.js AutoModelForSequenceClassification runs in a Web Worker. Screenshot: scratch-shots/offensive-index.png; responsive shots reports/responsive/screens/offensive-language-detection/*.png
rubric.notes: onnx-community/hate_speech_en-ONNX (IMSyPP), q8, single-label softmax over 4 classes.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — the four severity classes are one softmax and sum to ~100%: for the offensive sample, offensive 99.0% · acceptable 0.7% · inappropriate 0.1% · violent 0.1%. Discrimination confirmed across samples in a standalone pipeline run (acceptable→LABEL_0, offensive/violent→LABEL_2/3). Output shape + semantics correct, not fake.
rubric.notes: LABEL_0..3 mapped to the model card's documented acceptable/inappropriate/offensive/violent.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/hate_speech_en-ONNX linked to card), task (text-classification), backend (WASM), and dtype (q8) — matches conformance page-text/source assertions. Backend readout showed WASM at run time.

Agent verdict — fresh profile showed 'Download model (~109 MB)'; after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'Model ready — running locally on your device.' with a 'Clear cached model' control. transformers.js fetches THROUGH Cache Storage, so a returning visit auto-inits with no Load click. Uses lib/model-loader.js (no hand-rolled button).

Loader status is a role=status aria-live region (checking → initialising → ready); download progress streams to a <progress>; failures surface to a .status.err region with a Retry path; scoring errors write to #runStatus.err. Runs on the WASM path (no GPU) — the headless no-GPU run produced real output, so it just works without WebGPU.

Agent verdict — textarea, four sample chips (min 34px, keyboard-operable buttons), the offensive-or-worse threshold slider (re-renders verdict live), and the loader Download/Clear buttons all operable; verified on the index + basics/practical/wild pages. No dead controls.
Agent verdict — the visible bars/verdict are produced by the on-device worker run (offensive 99.0% for the hostile sample; real softmax). No canned result anywhere.

Conformance images-have-alt (no content images) and buttons-have-names pass; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow + aria-label; loader status is role=status aria-live. Design-system contrast (light+dark) inherited from public/styles.css.

Agent matrix pass — desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both automated-clean (no horizontal overflow, network+console clean) and screenshots read: header, at-a-glance table, chips, slider, bars all legible and wrapped; queue rows use overflow-wrap:anywhere so long comments don't overflow. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/offensive-language-detection/{desktop,mobile}.png

Agent verdict — all inference (tokenizer → model → softmax) runs in a Web Worker; the batch triage loop runs in the worker so the main thread never blocks; input is debounced 350 ms. Main thread stayed responsive during load; single-item latency ~77 ms.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker batch loop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy synchronous work off the interaction path; break up long loops so the browser can handle input.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js); the batch-triage loop over many comments runs inside the worker, so the control UI never blocks; text input is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless: 77 ms single score, main-thread eval responsive during load, 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcements aria-live; label every control
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/progress/error state in a live region; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live loader, labelled slider + chips + textarea, probability bars as role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use intrinsic sizing + flex-wrap + max-inline-size so panels reflow without horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — bars/thresh use max-inline-size:560px, chip rows flex-wrap, queue text uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; verified no overflow at 360px and 1280px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix desktop+mobile no-horizontal-overflow pass; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: cross document view transitions navigation between catalogue and page
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from the shared public/styles.css design system; crumbs link back to the catalogue and model page.

openQuestions: The IMSyPP config ships only generic LABEL_0..3; the acceptable/inappropriate/offensive/violent mapping is documented on the model card but pinned in worker.js — re-verify if upstream relabels.
Model was trained on English YouTube comments; recall on other registers (formal text, other Englishes) is untested.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a per-severity action config demo (different downstream action per rung: nudge on inappropriate, queue on offensive, block+report on violent).

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for offensive-language-detection (IMSyPP hate_speech_en, ~109 MB, WASM q8). Real on-device softmax verified in headless Chrome (offensive 99.0%, 77 ms), auto-init-on-cached and honest states confirmed, desktop+mobile screenshots read (no overflow, legible, operable), console clean. Framed honestly as an imperfect moderation signal with a see-inside softmax-vs-sigmoid contrast against toxicity.
